prerequisites: A Metricool account on an Advanced or Custom plan, since API access is only available on those plans.
At least one Brand (blog) connected in Metricool whose numeric blog ID you can supply as blog_ids.
Your Metricool user ID (user_id), required on every API call alongside the blog ID.
An API access token (user_token) generated from Account Settings > API.

authMethods.name: API token (X-Mc-Auth header)
authMethods.summary: Metricool authenticates with a single API access token. Provide it as the user_token secret; the connector sends it in the custom HTTP header X-Mc-Auth. The token is available on Advanced and Custom plans and is copied from Account Settings > API.

setupSteps.title: Confirm your plan supports API access
setupSteps.body: The Metricool API is only available on Advanced and Custom plans. Verify your account is on one of these plans before continuing, otherwise the API tab and token will not be available.

setupSteps.title: Generate and copy your API access token
setupSteps.body: In Metricool, go to Account Settings > API and copy your API access token. Use this value for the user_token field.

setupSteps.title: Find your user ID and blog ID
setupSteps.body: Every Metricool API call requires a userId and a blogId. Set user_id to your Metricool user ID and blog_ids to the ID(s) of the Brand(s) you want to sync. You can identify these IDs by inspecting the API endpoints used in your account (for example via the browser inspector while viewing Analytics, Planner, or Ads).

setupSteps.title: Set an optional date range
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date and end_date to bound the time range of metrics retrieved. Leave them empty to use the connector's default range.
